title: Marketing 90-day Prioritization
summary: Michael Donovan presented a 90-day game plan focusing on execution, rhythm, and outcomes like revenue and leads. Vinnie Micciche expressed frustration over the company's messaging, which he believes should reflect an "evolution" towards AI and automations, not a "new motion," and accused Michael of not critically reviewing content for a hyper-personalized approach. The team agreed on four core messaging building blocks: inbound, outbound, co-pilot/bot chat, and conversational AI/analytics. Vinnie instructed the team to enhance the existing website content for the upcoming NAEDC event rather than waiting for a new site.

decisions.description: Agreed on four core messaging building blocks for marketing efforts.
Enhance existing website content immediately instead of waiting for a new site.
Vinnie will reshare marketing documents for collective review by the team.

actionItems.task: Share three marketing documents again for collective review.
Enhance existing website content to align with current messaging.

expectedOutcome.description: Website reflects current messaging and AI evolution.

actionItems.task: Work on how to sell and integrate Sophia outbound capabilities.

expectedOutcome.description: Clear sales and integration plan for Sophia outbound.

actionItems.task: Figure out how to get data from non-Strawlet dealers for Sophia.

expectedOutcome.description: Method for integrating external dealer data for Sophia.

actionItems.task: Guide Michael to churn out great marketing content.
Share the 140 pages of content with Vinnie.

expectedOutcome.description: Vinnie reviews the content for messaging alignment.

tensions: Vinnie's frustration that company messaging is not understood or collaboratively reviewed.
Vinnie accused Michael of not critically reviewing content for appropriate conversational approach.
Disagreement between Vinnie and Michael on 'evolution' versus 'new motion' for strategic shift.
Vinnie's frustration with Michael and Z for selecting 'top one' content without critical thought.
Vinnie's frustration over Michael's comment about ChatGPT printouts, implying lack of effort.
Vinnie's perception that Michael disregards his input and prefers his own way.
Michael's failure to share 140 pages of content with Vinnie as requested.

contributions.description: Presented a high-level 90-day game plan document.
Emphasized company's evolution from service BDC to tech company.
Messaging needs to reflect move towards AI, automations, and efficiencies.
Instructed team to enhance existing website content for NAEDC.
Advocated for grassroots, hyper-personalized, conversational messaging for engagement.
Sought clarity on four core messaging building blocks (inbound, outbound, co-pilot, AI/analytics).
Highlighted Sophia outbound as a huge efficiency creating internal and external value.
Explained inbound service (Vita) is primarily for churn reasons.
Proposed real-time human capabilities to back up robots going sideways.
Mentioned 'dealer brain' for capturing all conversations in one place.
Proposed structuring messaging into three lanes based on dealer mindset and content.
